properties.remark: STREET FLOODING REPORTED IN THE COMMINITY OF HON.
WATER WAS OVER THE ROAD AT JACKSONVILLE ON HWY. 67/167.
WATER WAS OVER THE ROAD AT CAMP ROBINSON ON NEBRASKA AVENUE BETWEEN 10TH AND 14TH STREETS.
THE PUBLIC REPORTED THUNDERSTORMS PRODUCING HEAVY RAIN ON I-30 BETWEEN ARKANSAS RIVER BRIDGE AND I-630, PUBLIC REPORTED PONDING OF WATER AND MUD FLOW OVER THE INTERSTATE DUE TO CONSTRUCTION.
A FEW LARGE TREES DOWN IN THE AREA. ONE HOME WAS DAMAGED BY A SNAPPED TREE.
ROOF TORN OFF OF BARN IN HECTOR, AR. ROOF DEBRIS IS BLOCKING PINE STREET.
HIGHWAY 7 AND EAST H STREET IN RUSSELLVILLE, AR BLOCKED BY DOWNED POWER LINES.
MEDIA REPORT OF MULTIPLE TREES AND POWERLINES DOWN.
MULTIPLE TREES DOWN IN CLINTON, AR AND SEVERAL HOMES WITHOUT POWER.
A FEW LARGE TREES DOWN IN THE AREA.
SEVERAL TREES DOWN ACROSS NORTHERN PARTS OF CLEBURNE COUNTY.
MULTIPLE TREES DOWN IN BATESVILLE, AR.
ROOF BLOWN OFF A DUPLEX IN BIGGERS.
TREE FELL ON HOUSE NORTH OF POCAHONTAS. ALSO ROOF BLOWN OFF FACTORY BUILDING IN POCAHONTAS. NUMEROUS POWER LINES ARE DOWN.
ROOF BLOWN OFF TWO HOUESS IN MAYNARD.
A ROOF PARTIALLY BLOWN OFF OF A MOBILE HOME AND SEVERAL TREES AND POWER LINES DOWN ON CYPRESS STREET IN SEDGWICK, AR.
REPORT OF A TREE THAT HAS FALLEN ON A HOUSE IN SEDGWICK, AR.

THIS TORNADO STARTED IN WEST CENTRAL POPE COUNTY, JUST NORTHEAST OF AUGSBURG, AND TRACKED NORTHEAST FOR APPROXIMATELY 16 MILES, BEFORE DISSIPATING IN CENTRAL POPE COUNTY. THERE WAS MOSTLY TREE DAMAGE. THERE WAS SOME STRUCTURAL DAMAGE, WITH THE WORST DAMAGE TO A HOME 1.1 MILE WEST-NORTHWEST OF PLEASANT VALLEY (EXTENSIVE ROOF DAMAGE). THE SLIDING DOORS (THREE OF THEM) ON A VOLUNTEER FIRE DEPARTMENT WERE ALSO BLOWN IN ABOUT 0.8 MILE NORTH OF PLEASANT VALLEY ALONG HIGHWAY 7.
TREES DOWN FROM A CONFIRMED TORNADO NEAR THE RUSHING ROAD AREA APPROXIMATELY 3 MILES NORTH OF DOVER, AR.
TORNADO VIDEO POSTED ON SOCIAL MEDIA WEST OF HECTOR. TREES WERE DOWN AND DAMAGE REPORTED AT THE MARTIN TOWNSHIP FIRE DEPARTMENT.
TREES FELL ON A HOME ON WALNUT VALLEY ROAD AND ON A HOME ON SARA PAGE ROAD. THERE WERE NO INJURIES REPORTED.

THIS TORNADO STARTED SOUTHEAST OF DALTON IN RANDOLPH COUNTY. SEVERAL SMALL OUTBUILDINGS WERE DESTROYED, THERE WERE SOFTWOOD, SHALLOW ROOTED TREES UPROOTED, AND THERE WERE CHICKEN COOPS THAT WERE EITHER COMPLETELY OR PARTIALLY DESTROYED AS IT TRACKED NORTHEAST FOR APPROXIMATELY 2.5 MILES BEFORE DISSIPATING.
WIDESPREAD TREE DAMAGE IN THE AREA AS WELL AS DAMAGE TO A FEW BARNS.

THIS TORNADO STARTED IN THE SOUTHEASTERN CORNER OF SEARCY COUNTY. THE TORNADO UPROOTED SEVERAL TREES ALONG ITS PATH, ALONG WITH RIPPING ROOF PANELS OFF OF A BARN, AND DAMAGING OTHERS AS IT TRACKED NORTHEAST FOR APPROXIMATELY 2.5 MILES AND CROSSING INTO WESTERN STONE COUNTY, BEFORE DISSIPATING JUST NORTH OF FLAG.
POWER LINES AND TREES WERE BLOWN DOWN ON JIMMY CREEK ROAD.
POWER LINES AND TREES WERE BLOWN DOWN ON RAMSEY CEMETERY ROAD.
POWER LINES AND TREES WERE BLOWN DOWN ON NORTH RIDGEVIEW DRIVE.
TWO BARNS ON FINLEY CREEK ROAD SUSTAINED DAMAGE, AND MULTIPLE MOBILE HOMES WERE BLOWN OFF OF THEIR BLOCKS ON PINEY COVE ROAD.

*** 5 INJ *** THIS TORNADO STARTED IN CENTRAL IZARD COUNTY, JUST SOUTHEAST OF MELBOURNE, AND TRACKED NORTHEAST FOR 11 MILES, CROSSING INTO WESTERN SHARP COUNTY, AND DISSIPATING NORTHWEST OF EVENING SHADE. THERE WAS MOSTLY TREE DAMAGE. SEVERAL HOMES IN AND AROUND SAGE HAD ROOF DAMAGE, WITH A FEW BARNS AND SHEDS HEAVILY DAMAGED. THE WORST DAMAGE WAS 0.5 MILE EAST OF SAGE, WITH NUMEROUS TREES SNAPPED AND A HORSE BARN DESTROYED. FARTHER NORTHEAST, A MOBILE HOME WAS ROLLED, AND A PERSON WAS INJURED. A MODULAR HOME
*** 5 INJ *** A SEVERE THUNDERSTORM WITH A POSSIBLE TORNADO DAMAGED TWO HOUSES WITH POSSIBLY 5 INJURED. THIS WAS ON JACKSON SCHOOLHOUSE RD. 3 ENE OF ZION.
TRAINED STORM CHASERS REPORTED POWER FLASHES AND TREE DEBRIS ACROSS HIGHWAY 167 APPROXIMATELY 3 MILES NORTH OF EVENING SHADE, AR. THIS WAS POSSIBLY A TORNADO.
TRAINED STORM CHASERS REPORTED POWER FLASHES AND TREE DEBRIS ACROSS HIGHWAY 167 APPROXIMATELY 3 MILES NORTH OF EVENING SHADE, AR. THIS WAS POSSIBLY A TORNADO.
TRAINED STORM SPOTTER REPORT OF 62 MPH WIND GUST WITH TREES AND POWER LINES DOWN AT THE JUNCTION OF HIGHWAY 167 AND BEAR VALLEY ROAD ABOUT 5 MILES NORTH OF EVENING SHADE, AR.
SEVERAL POWER POLES KNOCKED DOWN ALONG WITH DAMAGE TO POULTRY HOUSES AND TWO STRUCTURES.

THIS TORNADO STARTED SOUTHEAST OF DALTON IN RANDOLPH COUNTY. SEVERAL SMALL OUTBUILDINGS WERE DESTROYED, THERE WERE SOFTWOOD, SHALLOW ROOTED TREES UPROOTED, AND THERE WERE CHICKEN COOPS THAT WERE EITHER COMPLETELY OR PARTIALLY DESTROYED AS IT TRACKED NORTHEAST FOR APPROXIMATELY 2.5 MILES BEFORE DISSIPATING. *REISSUED WITH CORRECTED BEGINNING TIME BASED ON RADAR DATA.

SOCIAL MEDIA PICTURES OF BARNS AND CHICKEN HOUSES WITH ROOF DAMAGE AND DOWNED TREES NEAR GLENDALE IN LINCOLN COUNTY. THE DAMAGE MAY BE FROM A TORNADO AS IT IS IN THE SAME TRACK AS THE PARENT SUPERCELL THAT PRODUCED TORNADO DAMAGE FURTHER NORTHEAST IN ARKANSAS COUNTY. LOCATION AND TIME ARE PRELIMINARY AND ESTIMATED FROM RADAR AND GOOGLE EARTH IMAGERY.

THIS TORNADO BEGAN IN WESTERN LINCOLN COUNTY, JUST SOUTH OF GLENDALE, AND TRACKED EAST-NORTHEAST FOR 9 MILES BEFORE DISSIPATING OVER US HWY 425, JUST SOUTH OF YORKTOWN. THE TORNADO INITIALLY DAMAGED SOME POULTRY HOUSES NEAR THE START OF THE PATH WITH THE REST OF THE DAMAGE LIMITED TO TREES.

THIS TORNADO STARTED IN SOUTHWEST ARKANSAS COUNTY, OR JUST WEST OF LUMSDENS RESERVOIR, AND TRACKED NORTHEAST FOR 7 MILES, MOVING ALONG JACOBS LAKE, AND DISSIPATING JUST BEFORE US HWY 165, IN BETWEEN DEWITT AND GILLETT. PRIMARILY DAMAGE TO TREES ALONG THIS PATH. LIMITED ACCESS TO PORTIONS OF THE TRACK DUE TO NEARBY LAKE AND PROPERTY ACCESS.
TREES WERE BLOWN DOWN ON HIGHWAY 1 5 MILES SOUTH OF DEWITT.
HOT SPRINGS POLICE DEPARTMENT REPORTED APPROXIMATELY A FOOT OF WATER OVER MARTIN LUTHER KING BYPASS ROAD IN BETWEEN HIGDON FERRY AND CENTRAL AVE.

A SURVEY TEAM FROM THE NWS IN LITTLE ROCK FOUND DAMAGE CONSISTENT WITH AN EF1 TORNADO NEAR HARMONY IN JOHNSON COUNTY. MORE INFORMATION WILL BE FORTHCOMING ONCE THE TEAM FINISHES THE ASSESSMENT.
NUMEROUS TREES BLOWN DOWN...ALONG WITH AN OLD CHICKEN HOUSE DAMAGED.

NWS SURVEY REVEALED THIS TORNADO STARTED EAST OF SCRANTON, JUST SW OF THE INTERSECTION OF AR HWY 197 AND STONEY POINT RD. SIG. DAMAGE WAS OBSERVED TO A METAL BARN, ALONG WITH ROOF DAMAGE TO A MOBILE HOME. SEVERAL TREES WERE SNAPPED/UPROOTED IN THIS AREA AND JUST NE. MORE TREE DAMAGE WAS NOTED AS THE TORNADO MOVED NE THROUGH SOME FOREST, SNAPPING/UPROOTING MANY TREES, CROSSING CANE CREEK RECREATION RD. THE TORNADO LIFTED JUST NORTH OF DUBLIN RD WEST OF THE JONES RD INTERSECTION.
REPORTED BY FIRE DEPARTMENT AND RELAYED BY PERRY COUNTY OEM.

THIS TORNADO STARTED IN THE NORTHERN EDGE OF CAMP ROBINSON ARMY BASE JUST SOUTHEAST OF THE INTERSECTION OF AR HWY 89 AND CLINTON RD., EVIDENT IN AREAL FOOTAGE. THE TORNADO TRAVELED GENERALLY EAST-SOUTHEAST, MAINLY UPROOTING TREES AND BLOWING DOWN TREE LIMBS. THIS TORNADO CROSSED CATO RD NORTH OF THE COMMUNITY OF CATO, UPROOTING MORE TREES. THE TORNADO LIFTED NEAR THE INTERSECTION OF BATESVILLE PIKE AND FORTSON RD.
SIX WINDOWS WERE BROKEN IN A HOUSE, WITH SEVERE DAMAGE TO SIDING AND LIKELY ROOF DAMAGE.

TREES FALLEN ON HOMES AND ACROSS ROADS, WINDOWS DAMAGED, POWER OUTAGES

A LARGE POLE BARN WAS COMPLETELY DESTROYED AND ELECTRICAL TRANSMISSION LINE TOWERS WERE KNOCKED DOWN. THE LIKELY CAUSE OF DAMAGE THAT INITIATED THE COLLAPSE OF THE ELECTRICAL TRANSMISSION LINE TOWERS WAS A LARGE LOFTED SHIPPING CONTAINER THAT COLLIDED WITH THE BASE OF ONE OF THE TOWERS. OTHER DAMAGE INCLUDED DOWNED SMALLER POWER POLES AND MINOR ROOF DAMAGE TO A HOME.

THIS POWERFUL MICROBURST DESTROYED A LARGE, WELL-BUILT METAL STORAGE BUILDING. THE BUILDING WAS ANCHORED AT 10 POINTS WITH CONCRETE AROUND EACH POST, AND IT APPEARS INITIAL DAMAGE ON ONE SIDE OF THE BUILDING CAUSED THE INTERIOR OF THE BUILDING TO BECOME EXPOSED TO THE WIND. THE STRUCTURAL SOUNDNESS OF THE BUILDING ALLOWED WIND ENERGY TO BUILD UP INSIDE UNTIL THE ENTIRE STRUCTURE WAS VIOLENTLY LIFTED FROM THE GROUND. DEBRIS WAS CARRIED APPROXIMATELY 700 YARDS DOWNSTREAM.
LARGE ELECTRICAL TRANSMISSION TOWERS DOWN AND SOME POWER POLES.

THIS BRIEF TORNADO DEVELOPED ALONG A STRONG REAR FLANK DOWNDRAFT SURGE THAT SWEPT ACROSS AREAS IMMEDIATELY SOUTHEAST OF WICKES, AR. SEVERAL CHICKEN HOUSES IMMEDIATELY SOUTH OF HWY 278 SUSTAINED VARYING DEGREES OF DAMAGE -- FROM ROOF PANELS STRIPPED OFF MOST STRUCTURES TO WALLS COLLAPSED ON AT LEAST ONE STRUCTURE. THE TORNADO QUICKLY MOVED NORTHEAST CAUSING ADDITIONAL TREE DAMAGE ALONG HWY 278 BEFORE DISSIPATING. SURROUNDING TREE DAMAGE WEST OF THE TRACK SUGGESTED STRONG INFLOW INTO THE TORNADO
THE ROOF OF A VACANT HOUSE LOCATED ON LAKE NORFORK DRIVE COLLAPSED. A COLLAPSED ROOF AT AN OUTDOOR BOAT STORAGE BUSINESS LOCATED ON HWY 62 EAST RESULTED DAMAGE TO SEVERAL BOATS.
FLASH FLOODING WAS OBSERVED ALONG MAIN STREET, TEMPORARILY CLOSING PORTIONS OF THE ROAD.
SEVERAL CHICKEN HOUSES AND BARNS SUSTAINED DAMAGE. A NUMBER OF TREES WERE ALSO BLOWN DOWN. TIME WAS ESTIMATED BY RADAR.
NUMEROUS TREES WERE BLOWN DOWN THROUGHOUT THE COUNTY. DOWNED TREES TEMPORARILY BLOCKED HWY 62 EAST, HWY 5 SOUTH, AND HWY 178 WEST, AS WELL AS NUMEROUS COUNTY ROADS. THIS IS A DELAYED REPORT.

THE TORNADO BEGAN ALONG LITTLE TEXAS ROAD SOUTH OF HWY 28 IN YELL COUNTY. SEVERAL PINE AND HARDWOOD TREES WERE EITHER SNAPPED OR UPROOTED ALONG LITTLE TEXAS ROAD AND BILLINGSLEY LANE. AS THE TORNADO MOVED NORTHEAST AND CROSSED HWY 28 NEAR GAFFORD CREEK, A CARPORT WAS DESTROYED BUT THE HOME RECEIVED NO DAMAGE. ADDITIONAL TREE DAMAGE OCCURRED AS THE TORNADO CROSSED GAFFORD CREEK WITH THE TORNADO DISSIPATING SHORTLY AFTER NEAR BLUFF ROAD.
TREES AND POWERLINES DOWN.

NWS SURVEY REVEALED AN EF1 TORNADO SOUTHEAST OF CURTIS. THIS TORNADO STARTED JUST SOUTHWEST OF VADEN ROAD, CONTINUING NORTHEAST ACROSS 2ND ROAD TOWARDS NEWTON AND COPELAND RIDGE ROADS. MAINLY TREE DAMAGE WAS NOTED, EITHER UPROOTED OR SNAPPED. AN OLD CHURCH BUILDING WAS KNOCKED JUST SLIGHTLY OFF THE BLOCK FOUNDATION. THIS TORNADO CONTINUED NORTHEAST, EVENTUALLY CROSSING OPEN BANKS ROAD NEAR THE OUACHITA RIVER. TORNADO LIFTED JUST ACROSS THE OUACHITA RIVER FROM THIS LOCATION.

THE TORNADO BEGAN AT THE BASE OF KIRBY MOUNTAIN JUST TO THE EAST OF HIGHWAY 27 AND SOUTH OF MITCHELL ROAD. ALONG THE PATH OF THE TORNADO, A BARN WAS COMPLETELY DESTROYED. A METAL OUTBUILDING HAD MINOR ROOF DAMAGE. NUMEROUS TREES WERE SNAPPED OR UPROOTED. THE TORNADO ENDED ALONG AND JUST NORTH OF HIGHWAY 84 EAST OF KIRBY.

THE TORNADO BEGAN JUST NORTH OF NORMAN HILL ROAD AT KINGSTON ROAD AND TRACKED NORTHEAST ALONG KINGSTON ROAD. THIS TORNADO SNAPPED TREES ADJACENT TO THE ROAD AND REMOVED MUCH OF THE ROOFING MATERIAL FROM A HOME. A SMALL OUTBUILDING WAS DESTROYED ON COVEY RISE LANE. THE TORNADO CONTINUED TO MOVE NORTHEAST BEFORE DISSIPATING NEAR PEELER GAP ROAD NORTH OF COUNTY ROAD 728. TREES WERE SNAPPED ALONG THE PATH FROM COVEY RISE LANE TO THE ENDING POINT.

NWS SURVEY REVEALED AN EF1 TORNADO SOUTHEAST OF CURTIS. THIS TORNADO STARTED JUST SOUTHWEST OF VADEN ROAD, CONTINUING NORTHEAST ACROSS 2ND ROAD TOWARDS NEWTON AND COPELAND RIDGE ROADS. MAINLY TREE DAMAGE WAS NOTED, EITHER UPROOTED OR SNAPPED. AN OLD CHURCH BUILDING WAS KNOCKED JUST SLIGHTLY OFF THE BLOCK FOUNDATION. THIS TORNADO CONTINUED NORTHEAST, EVENTUALLY CROSSING OPEN BANKS ROAD NEAR THE OUACHITA RIVER. THE TORNADO CROSSED AND PARALLELED THE RIVER FOR A COUPLE OF MILES BEFORE DISSIPATING.

NWS SURVEY REVEALED AN EF1 TORNADO SOUTHEAST OF CURTIS. THIS TORNADO STARTED JUST SOUTHWEST OF VADEN ROAD, CONTINUING NORTHEAST ACROSS 2ND ROAD TOWARDS NEWTON AND COPELAND RIDGE ROADS. MAINLY TREE DAMAGE WAS NOTED, EITHER UPROOTED OR SNAPPED. AN OLD CHURCH BUILDING WAS KNOCKED JUST SLIGHTLY OFF THE BLOCK FOUNDATION. THIS TORNADO CONTINUED NORTHEAST, EVENTUALLY CROSSING OPEN BANKS ROAD NEAR THE OUACHITA RIVER. THE TORNADO CROSSED AND PARALLELED THE RIVER FOR A COUPLE OF MILES BEFORE DISSIPATING.

NWS SURVEY REVEALED AN EF2 TORNADO SOUTHEAST OF SARDIS. THIS TORNADO STARTED ON CARDINAL CIRCLE, UPROOTING OR SNAPPING TREES AS IT MOVED NORTHEAST OVER WEST FAIRVIEW ROAD AND LONG DRIVE. THE WORST DAMAGE WAS NOTED ALONG PRUETT ROAD, WHERE SEVERAL HOMES WERE DAMAGED, SOME WHICH LOST THEIR ROOFS. THE TORNADO CONTINUED NORTHEAST OVER EAST SARDIS ROAD, DAMAGING MORE TREES. THE TORNADO LIFTED NEAR THE INTERSECTION OF EAST SARDIS ROAD AND STAN COVE.
